Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award, For Judgment In Conformance Thereith And For Motion For Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Action And To Expunge Plaintiffs Mechanics Lien
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MABCO INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL BAGATELOS, VERA KOPILENKO, AND BO PITSKER's Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award, For Judgment In Conformance Therewith And For Motion For Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Action And To Expunge Plaintiffs Mechanics Lien.

Defendants' Petition for an Order Confirming Arbitration Award and for Judgment In Conformance Therewith and their Motion For Dismissal of Plaintiff's Action and To Expunge Plaintiff's Mechanic's Lien came on for hearing in this Department on June 18, 2025 at 9:00 am pursuant to notice. No opposition to the Petition or the Motion has been filed. Accordingly, the motion is GRANTED. Counsel's request for $8,820.00 in attorneys fees and costs is also GRANTED.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: John True; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/JT)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2023 Continued To Jul-24-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Dec-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jaime Jiyoon Kim, Esq., LK PROFESSIONAL LAW GROUP (213) 788-3588 Attorneys for Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant, STREAMLINED BUILDING, INC.
S. Patrick Miller, Esq., STUHR & MILLER, LLP, 415-585-2122, Attorney for Defendants MABCO INVESTMENTS, LLC

Hearing held. ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MABCO INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL BAGATELOS, VERA KOPILENKO, BO PITSKER MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Defendant Mabco Investments, LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted in part.

Plaintiff Streamlined Building Inc. contracted with defendants to provide construction services for a San Francisco property. The parties to the contract were Streamlined; Mabco; Mabco president, Michael Bagetelos; and property owners, Vera Kopilenko and Bo Pitsko. (Mot. Exh. 1) In 2021, Streamlined initiated this action against Mabco, Bagatelos, Kopilenko, and Pitsko for breach of contract and related claims. In March 2023, defendants filed a cross-complaint that added cross-defendants Paul Johnson; Streamline General Building, Inc. ("SGBI"); KSJ Development, Inc.; Hyunuk Kang; Inncon Design, LLC; and Chiu Chiu Chow. Mabco now moves to compel arbitration based on the arbitration clause in the original construction contract.

Mabco has met its burden of showing a valid arbitration agreement by attaching a copy of the signed construction contract. (Mot. Exh. 1, provision 32 "Dispute Resolution"; Iyere v. Wise Auto Group (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 747, 755.) Streamlined raises no substantive arguments to invalidate the agreement, and it is thus enforceable as to the original contracting parties - Streamlined, Mabco, Bagetelos, Kopilenko, and Pitsko.

Next, Mabco argues the non-signatory cross-defendants should be compelled to arbitrate under theories of agency, alter ego, equitable estoppel, and third-party beneficiary. (See Benaroya v. Willis (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th 462, 469.) Kang is an officer of Streamlined who benefitted financially from providing construction services. (See RJN, Exh. A-1, A-2.) Kang can be compelled to arbitrate as an agent. (See, e.g., RN Solution, Inc. v. Catholic Healthcare West (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1511, 1520.) Though Johnson is also an officer of Streamlined, there is no evidence he has been served with the cross-complaint; thus, compelling him to arbitration is premature. Lastly, Mabco has not shown on this record that SGBI, KSJ, Inncon, and Chow have a connection to the signatory parties such that they can be compelled to arbitrate.

In sum, Streamlined, Mabco, Bagetelos, Kopilenko, Pitsko, and Kang are ordered to arbitrate. The action is stayed as to all defendants pending arbitration. (CCP 1281.4.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
